About

I'm a recent Biomedical Engineering graduate from Rensselaer Polytechnic Institute (May 2026), with a minor in Electrical Engineering and Public Health, specializing in Medical Devices and Bioimaging/Bioinstrumentation. I'm actively pursuing roles in biomedical field service engineering and medical device testing, where I can apply my technical skills in instrumentation, hardware, and device validation in a clinical or industry setting.

Recent work spans a validated diffusion phantom for cervical and endometrial cancer MRI built with the Icahn School of Medicine at Mount Sinai, a U-Net trained from scratch to segment breast cancer histopathology slides, and design verification testing on dry electrode films with the US Army's DEVCOM. I also build the embedded and robotic systems underneath — a Raspberry Pi-based RFID access control system for my lab, and a full ROS 2 Nav2 stack for an autonomous delivery robot. I like problems that need a steady hand at the bench and rigor at the keyboard — CAD, FEA, circuit design, embedded systems, and deep learning, all in service of devices clinicians can trust.

Experience
Sept '25 – May '26

Undergraduate Research Assistant

MercerX Lab
  • Developing a Raspberry Pi-based embedded access control system integrating an ARTS (Attendance Recording and Tracking System) RFID card reader, enforcing training-based equipment authorization through hardware-software co-design.
  • Implementing Python scheduling and user-permission logic for a multi-user lab environment; collaborating with lab technicians to define system design and safety requirements.
May – Aug '24

Plastics Engineering Research Assistant

UMass Lowell
  • Worked with the US Army (DEVCOM) and the Fabric Discovery Center to conduct design verification testing on dry electrode film samples — evaluating durability, strain, abrasion resistance, and BioPac monitoring performance against design specifications.
  • Set up, calibrated, and operated test instruments, including BioPac monitoring systems; recorded test protocols and produced data reports supporting repeated design cycles following medical-grade device development standards.
Jun – Aug '23

Software Engineering Intern

Burst.com
  • Upgraded a Python-based AWS Lambda that powered search via Elasticsearch and its associated dependencies.
  • Supported migration from ELK 5 to ELK 8, owning the manual migration of Kibana saved searches, visualizations, and dashboards affected by breaking changes in the newer version.
  • Performed data analysis on ELK objects, cataloging all objects in a CSV to facilitate execution of the migration.
Projects
Jul '26 – Present In Progress

Biopotential (ECG/EMG) Acquisition Board

INA333 · KiCad · Analog Front-End
  • Designing and building a custom biopotential acquisition device from schematic to bring-up, centered on an INA333 instrumentation amplifier with a right-leg-drive circuit for common-mode noise rejection.
  • Implementing active high-pass and low-pass filtering (Sallen-Key topology) to isolate the ECG/EMG signal band ahead of digitization.
  • Laying out a full 2-layer PCB in KiCad with careful analog/digital ground separation for signal integrity, then validating captured waveforms against a reference ECG dataset.
Sept '25 – May '26 Completed

Diffusion Phantom for Cervical & Endometrial Cancer MRI

Senior Capstone · with Icahn School of Medicine at Mount Sinai
  • Developed a cervical diffusion phantom, iterating on material concentrations to create a controlled, validated range of diffusivity values simulating cancer-associated tissue.
  • Applied DV methods and quantitative biomarker validation protocols as a standard tool for evaluating DW-MRI imaging systems.
  • Troubleshot agarose gel concentration inconsistencies, establishing a reproducible concentration–diffusivity correlation.
Feb – Apr '26 Completed

Breast Cancer Histopathology Segmentation (BEETLE)

PyTorch · U-Net
  • Built a deep learning segmentation pipeline for breast cancer histopathology whole-slide images, training a U-Net from scratch to delineate clinically relevant tissue regions.
  • Preprocessed WSIs into ROI patches aligned to pathologist annotations for supervised tumor-boundary detection.
Feb – Apr '26 Completed

Autonomous Food Delivery Robot

ROS 2 · Nav2 · SLAM
  • Built a 2D occupancy map via Cartographer SLAM; implemented AMCL localization and tuned EKF, PID, and DWB cost weights; resolved LiDAR noise and scan-matching failures.
  • Deployed a full ROS 2 Nav2 stack on a Yahboom mecanum-wheel robot, configuring an A* global planner and DWB local planner for real-time obstacle avoidance.
Mar '25 Completed

Cantilever Beam Velocity Estimator

Analog Circuits · MATLAB
  • Designed a dual-modality sensing system comparing an ADXL327 accelerometer (integration path) and a resistive strain gauge (differentiation path) to estimate beam tip velocity.
  • Fabricated analog signal-conditioning circuits (Wheatstone bridge, instrumentation diff amp, op-amp integrator/differentiator); processed multi-channel waveforms in MATLAB to validate estimates.
Nov '24 Completed

Structural Simulation of Tibia Under Multi-Mode Loading

SolidWorks · FEA
  • Modeled a hollow tibia in SolidWorks and ran FEA under compression, torsion, and bending to evaluate deformation and peak stress, informing implant loading analysis consistent with FDA / ISO 7206-4 frameworks.
  • Computed analytical stress/strain across varying elastic moduli and validated against FEA output.
